rationale.root_cause: They blamed an unsafe strcpy at a wrong line, but the actual flaw is the memcpy in CWE122_..._bad() using sizeof(*structCharVoid), which overwrites beyond charFirst into the pointers. They did not identify the struct-field overrun mechanism present in this file.
rationale.local_fix: They suggested replacing strcpy with strncpy, which does not apply here and does not change the erroneous memcpy size. The required fix is to copy only sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst) bytes into charFirst.
rationale.global_fix: They proposed general bans on strcpy/strcat and compiler flags, not a bounded field copy helper or typed buffer wrapper to prevent confusing struct size with field size. It does not address the root cause of using the struct size for the field copy.
